title: China's New AI Robot MOYA Feels Too Real (92% Human)
summary: A YouTube video analysis covering China's MOYA robot with lifelike skin and reactions alongside Boston Dynamics Atlas and Alibaba's Qwen-Robot. The creator argues humanoid robots are transitioning from staged demos to practical real-world tasks in factories, hospitals, and warehouses.
writeup: The video surveys recent humanoid robotics, centering on China's MOYA robot, which is presented with lifelike skin and reactions rated at 92% human, alongside Boston Dynamics Atlas and Alibaba's Qwen-Robot. The throughline is a claimed shift from staged demonstrations toward practical deployment in factories, hospitals, and warehouses. Supporting concepts include AI agents, spatial guidance, and test-time compute.
